title: Redland City Council Community Support Grant
summary: Round 1 for 2026/27 is scheduled for 27 July 2026 to 16 August 2026. Funding is up to $20,000 and applications are open quarterly.
action: Prepare applicant eligibility, ABN, insurance, quotes over $5,000, project plan, risk assessment, budget and reporting plan before 27 July.
status: Future round - Round 1 opens 27 July 2026 and closes 16 August 2026

title: Redland City Council Grants and Sponsorship
summary: Council keeps multiple grant streams and support pages. The portal should be checked before assuming Community Support is the best fit.
action: Scan council program pages monthly and before any local event, arts, environment or community application.
status: Council portal - current programs and round links

title: Arts Queensland Funding
summary: Arts Queensland separates funding for individuals and organisations. Check specific arts program pages for actual open dates before creating a grant notice.
action: Keep artist profile.md, aura.md, business-profile.md and project evidence ready before matching a round.
status: Program list

title: Gambling Community Benefit Fund
summary: Round 126 is closed. The next GCBF round date is still not announced while Queensland introduces a new online grants system. This Round 126 note applies only to GCBF.
action: Use the waiting period to confirm eligibility, auspice, quotes, public benefit, governance and reporting capacity.
status: Round 126 closed; next round date still not announced

title: Queensland Grants Finder
summary: Use the Queensland Grants Finder as a broad search layer, then confirm the program page and round dates from the administering agency.
action: Run a filtered state scan for sport, arts, environment, First Nations, business, disaster and community keywords monthly.
status: Search portal

title: Queensland Reconstruction Authority resilience funding programs
summary: QRA lists current resilience funding programs including QRRRP, DRFA efficiencies, Protecting Our Communities, North Queensland resilience and DRF.
action: Map the project to risk reduction, recovery, resilience or emergency readiness before choosing a funding path.
status: Current resilience funding programs listed

title: Queensland Sport and Recreation Funding
summary: Queensland sport funding lists Active Clubs, First Nations sport, grassroots infrastructure, inclusive facilities, recovery and active industry programs.
action: Keep a sport facility evidence folder: site control, quotes, plans, participation evidence, inclusion benefits and maintenance responsibility.
status: Program list - multiple sport and recovery streams

title: Queensland disaster grants and financial assistance
summary: Queensland disaster grants include emergency hardship, essential services, household contents, structural assistance and reconnection support. This is public support routing, not ordinary project funding.
action: Use for emergency notices and referrals after activated events; do not list as a generic project grant.
status: Support information - activated disaster support, not normal project funding

title: First Nations Clean Energy Advice Grants Round 1
summary: Open until 5:00pm AEST 3 September 2026, unless funding is allocated earlier. Grants are $5,000 to $80,000.
action: Confirm First Nations applicant eligibility, ABN, project authority, advice provider, FPIC framing and whether the project needs quotes over $40,000.
status: Open - closes 5:00pm AEST 3 September 2026, or earlier if funding is allocated

title: First Nations Digital Support Hub Program
summary: Full applications are due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026. This is likely a large lead-provider or partnership opportunity, not a casual small grant.
action: Triage immediately: confirm eligibility, lead applicant, delivery capacity, cultural authority, partners and whether the island project is a partner rather than applicant.
status: Open - full applications due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026

title: Indigenous.gov.au Grants
summary: Indigenous.gov.au currently lists open and ongoing grants across Country, economic development, health, digital inclusion, arts and local investment.
action: Check who is actually eligible before drafting. Cultural authority is not a writing detail; it is a project foundation.
status: Program list

title: Network of Digital Mentors Program
summary: Full applications are due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026. It could align with noticeboard, kiosks and community media, but only if eligibility and authority are real.
action: Treat as a rapid partner check, not a cold application: identify eligible lead, mentors, communities, training evidence and delivery budget.
status: Open - full applications due 5:00pm AEST 30 June 2026

title: PBC Capacity Building Grant Opportunity
summary: Indigenous.gov.au lists this as closing 30 June 2026. It is only relevant if the applicant is the right kind of native-title corporation or has a proper role.
action: Check applicant status and authority before drafting. Do not bend a community project into a PBC grant without governance fit.
status: Open - closes 30 June 2026

title: Queensland First Nations Grants and Funding
summary: Queensland lists multiple First Nations funding pathways including NAIDOC, languages, reconciliation, wellbeing, business, housing, arts, sport, Looking after Country and GenQ.
action: Use as a category scan, then confirm the live program page before drafting or notifying anyone.
status: Program list

title: Queensland First Nations housing support
summary: This is support-pathway context rather than a single open project grant. It helps separate renting, public/community housing, remote housing, emergency accommodation and aged-care housing issues.
action: Build a housing-specific evidence pack before looking for capital, maintenance, tenancy support or crisis-response funding.
status: Support information

title: Australian Government Health, Disability and Ageing grants and tenders
summary: The department points public health, disability and aged-care grants through GrantConnect and Community Grants Hub.
action: Prepare evidence of need, service authority, privacy boundaries, cultural safety and delivery partners before drafting.
status: Program information - public grants listed through GrantConnect

title: Community Grants Hub
summary: Community Grants Hub currently lists open grants and states GrantConnect is the authoritative grants information source.
action: Scan open grants, shortlist only genuine island-relevant opportunities, then confirm final rules on GrantConnect.
status: Open listings page - current rounds visible

title: GrantConnect
summary: GrantConnect is the authoritative Australian Government grants source, but may require a browser/manual check. Do not rely only on summaries for final eligibility.
action: Before drafting, capture the GrantConnect GO number, close date, eligibility, attachments and guidelines link.
status: Authoritative federal portal - JavaScript/manual check required

title: Modernised Multicultural Grants Program: Infrastructure for Multicultural Communities
summary: Batch 2 is open until 4:00pm AEDT 12 November 2026. Streams range from $20,000 to $1.5 million and require co-contribution and land ownership or approval evidence.
action: Only shortlist if the applicant is eligible, not-for-profit, infrastructure-ready and has landowner evidence plus co-contribution capacity.
status: Open - Batch 2 closes 4:00pm AEDT 12 November 2026

title: business.gov.au Grants and Programs Finder
summary: business.gov.au can filter by Queensland, industry, business structure, Indigenous business status, open/coming/closed, and needs such as AI, digital, disaster, equipment, events and training.
action: Use filters monthly and whenever a business-profile.md is created or updated.
status: Search portal - filters open, coming soon and closed programs

title: Island sponsorship and partner support watchlist
summary: Not every useful pathway is a government grant. Local support may be cash, in-kind, a venue, a letter, a noticeboard slot, a market table or a media partnership.
action: Build a local partner list beside each grant-ready project and record asks, offers and follow-up dates.
status: Local support list to build

title: UNOPS Small Grants
summary: UNOPS small grants information is a global programme-support pathway, not a quick local round. It suits long preparation and partnerships.
action: Treat as partnership development first, application writing second.
status: Global program information
